sanskrit: ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति। समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम्॥
transliteration: brahma-bhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śocati na kāṅkṣati | samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu mad-bhaktiṁ labhate parām ||
literal_meaning: One who has become one with Brahman is of joyful mind; he neither grieves nor craves, is equal toward all beings, and attains supreme devotion to Me.
contextual_meaning: A psyche that has integrated its fragmented parts (brahma-bhūta) experiences stable inner joy, loss of compulsive wanting or avoidance, and universal relatedness; from this secure base, mature relational devotion naturally emerges.
psychological_state: Secure-autonomous attachment; post-traumatic growth; integrated self-identity
trigger_scenario: After years of therapy and meditation, you notice you no longer panic over work emails or envy friends' success; you feel calmly connected to everyone and discover an effortless wish to serve something larger than yourself.
anchor_text: Rest in wholeness, love everyone equally, and let devotion bloom.
